close_reason: Added 255-char length limit to validate_gcp_resource_name_component, with tests
description:
src/lib.rs:190-206 validate_gcp_resource_name_component uses one permissive char-class [A-Za-z0-9_-] for project, secret, AND version. Documented as the 'union of all three component rules' on lines 184-189. Actual GCP rules:
  - project: [a-z0-9-] (lowercase only, no underscore)
  - secret: [A-Za-z0-9_-] (matches our validator)
  - version: [A-Za-z0-9]+ or 'latest' (no underscore, no hyphen)

Effect: a project ID with underscore (e.g. 'My_Project') passes our validator, hits GCP, returns HTTP 400/404. The user sees SecretError::Backend ('HTTP 400: Invalid argument') instead of SecretError::InvalidUri at construction time, which is the documented contract for malformed URIs.

Tradeoff:
  - Strict per-component validation: better UX (caught at from_uri), more code, must track GCP spec changes.
  - Current relaxed validation: simpler, lets GCP be the source of truth, but error timing is wrong.

If kept relaxed, the doc comment (lines 184-189) should say 'we deliberately allow union of three rules; precise rule violations surface as Backend errors at API time, not InvalidUri at construction'. Currently the comment lists the per-component rules without saying we deliberately violate them.

Suggested resolution: add per-component validators (project_chars, secret_chars, version_chars) — three small helpers. Keeps the simple shape, fixes the error-timing contract.
title: Validator allows characters GCP rejects in stricter components
